rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PHILIP M. EISENBERG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement Certification Of Settlement Class Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And Award Of Service Payment To Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement Certification Of Settlement Class Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And Award Of Service Payment To Plaintiffs Granted in large part. The fees awarded to class counsel are reduced to $600,000, which is 20% of the initial settlement fund and is a multiplier in excess of 1.4. While class counsel achieved excellent results, their hourly rates are quite high, class members are charities, the case was a very attractive one from the perspective of a class contingency lawyer, and a multiplier of 1.4 is sufficient to compensate for the litigation risks. Mr. Messick is not entitled to a service award, the service award for the Regents is reduced to $5000 and the service award to BBF is reduced to $1000. Class counsel is deemed to contest this tentative ruling to the extent the motion has not been granted in full and a hearing will be held accordingly. Class counsel is required to prepare a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling and bring it to the hearing. =302/

rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Bifurcate Issues From Trial On Liability And Damages
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANDREA DEBERRY Mtn To Bifurcate Issues From Trial On Liability And Damages The motion is denied without prejudice. The defendant may renew the motion before the trial judge. rulings.Case Title: LUIS GONZALEZ VS. ANDREA DEBERRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANDREA DEBERRY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. Plaintiff's worker's compensation case is not a bar to this action. Assuming without deciding that for the Cal-OSHA regulations do not apply under the household domestic service exception, there is still a triable issue whether Mr. Parsley, defendants' employee, breached his common law duty of care by failing to give plaintiff proper instructions and safety equipment and by providing a weathered rope to perform his duties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KIM KREIS et al VS. AMERICAN MULTI-CINEMA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition . Production Of Documents, Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS KIM KREIS and KATRINA KREIS' Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition, Production Of Documents, Monetary Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties agree that the motion regarding production of the IME report is moot since the report was recently produced. The motion to compel the PMK depositions noticed by Plaintiff is granted. The depositions shall occurr no later than September 19, 2012. The motion to compel production of Request Nos. 3 and 6 is granted. Defendant shall produce those documents at last one day prior to the commencement of the PMK depositions referenced above. Any documents withheld on privilege shall be identified on a log. All requests for sanctions are denied. (See Part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAENEAR YAP VS. VELO BDBI SUPPORT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT VELO BDBI SUPPORT, INC., BIKES DIRECT.COM MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross Complaint Denied. Granting the motion will inevitably delay the trial for at least six months. If defendants prevail at trial, there will be no need to pursue their indemnity claims. If they do not prevail, then they will still be able to bring those claims in a separate lawsuit. Defendants also fail to explain why they did not file their proposed cross-complaint at the time they filed their answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: COBE COLOR COSMETICS INC., A CORPORATION VS. WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT PREFERRED BANK, A CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action, sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the third cause of action, and overruled as to the claim for unauthorized payment of check. The face of the First Amended Complaint does not clearly and affirmatively show that any of plaintiffs' claims are time-barred. The first cause of action is preempted by the California Commercial Code. It is unlikely that leave to amend can cure the defect since the third cause of action already alleges violations of the applicable California Commercial Code section. The third cause of action, however, fails to allege Defendant Preferred Bank's duty to Plaintiff. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure the defect. The claim for unauthorized payment of check is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: COBE COLOR COSMETICS INC., A CORPORATION VS. WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. NAMED ERRONEOULSY AS WELLS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and fourth causes of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. The face of the First Amended Complaint does not clearly and affirmatively show that plaintiff's claims are time-barred. The first and fourth causes of action are preempted by the California Commercial Code. It is unlikely leave to amend can cure this defect since the second cause of action already alleges violations of the applicable California Commercial Code section. The second cause of action, however, fails to allege Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's duty to Plaintiff. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure the defect. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the requested documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: BRANDGENUITY LLC VS. ZYNGA INC., F/K/A ZYNGA GAME NETWORK, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 7, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT ZYNGA, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Plaintiff has properly pled a claim for goods and services rendered. Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. The terms of a contract are not a proper subject for judicial notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
